key_changes: Adds stamphog_review_inbox_prs boolean field to ReviewUserSettings (migration 0019), default off, alongside the existing review_inbox_prs toggle
Serializer exposes the new field plus a computed stamphog_connected flag that fails soft (returns False) if the cross-product Stamphog DB check errors

TaskRun receiver now resolves the acting reviewer once (_resolve_assigned_reviewer) and independently checks both toggles, dispatching review_hog's own review and/or stamphog's queue_inbox_pr_review facade on commit
Registers resolve_stamphog_acting_reviewer with stamphog's inbox hook registry at app-ready so the webhook leg can re-check the toggle without a circular import
New 'Let Stamphog review your Inbox PRs' switch in CodeReviewScene, disabled with a reason when Stamphog isn't connected but always interactive to opt out
Generated API schema/zod/mcp client types regenerated for the new fields (mechanical, attached for context)
New facade/inbox_hooks.py: import-light inversion-hook registry so stamphog can call back into review_hog's toggle resolver without a dependency cycle
facade/api.py: has_reviewable_repo_config (drives the UI's stamphog_connected flag) and queue_inbox_pr_review (fire-and-forget Celery dispatch for the receiver/inbox leg)

tasks.py: _inbox_rereview_carve_out positively identifies self-driving PRs on the webhook path (repo-native head, synced+enabled repo config, tasks-facade match, re-checked toggle) and bypasses bot-author/draft/review-mode/write-permission gates only for that carve-out; new process_inbox_pr_review Celery task implements the initial receiver-leg review with head-keyed dedupe and inbox provenance; dedicated dismissal copy for opted-out reviewers

temporal/activities.py: derives self_driving_review from the run's stamped provenance for the engine context, skips the author merged-PR fetch on inbox runs, and stamps analytics with stamphog_self_driving_review
logic/reviewer.py: threads the new self_driving_review flag into the sandboxed engine invocation context
products/tasks facade: new find_signal_implementation_run + SignalImplementationRunDTO, mirroring the webhook backstop's PR lookup and enforcing team scoping to positively identify self-driving runs
tach.toml: registers stamphog as a facade-only dependency for the modules now calling into it
Pipeline gains an additive, default-off self_driving flag that relaxes exactly two gates: the bot-author refusal and the draft prerequisite
review_local.py sets the flag from the hosted context JSON's self_driving_review key, defaulting closed so Action-shaped contexts are unaffected
reviewer.py renders a TRUSTED provenance block in the LLM prompt only when the flag is set, replacing human-author trust signals; empty string otherwise keeps every other prompt byte-identical
New 'self-driving inbox carve-out' invariant section in AGENTS.md documenting the narrow, positively-identified exception to the bot-author refusal and the gates it does/doesn't bypass
README.md documents the non-webhook entry point via the inbox receiver's queue_inbox_pr_review facade call
